test_suite.details: node test.js: 3513 passed, 0 failed. Covers wins.json schema, HTML output consistency, internal links, build pipeline, sections.json schema, prose content, prediction panels (79 panels: 12 tombstone, 67 mined, 10 operational), Phase A design refresh.

internal_anchors.details: Covered by test.js Section 3 (Internal Links) which passed. No additional broken anchors found.

tab_structure.details: Covered by test.js. Heading hierarchy and sectionNav chain verified during build (build.js html regenerated cleanly). Active default tab = overview.

external_links.details: Checked 75 non-citation external URLs (NOAA, USGS, NASA, ESA, INTERMAGNET, CHIME, GitHub, etc.). All 200/3xx except PO.DAAC (NASA JPL) which timed out twice at 12s and 20s. Recurring infra issue across recent runs — moderate, not alarming. Atoptics blog URLs timed out on first attempt but returned 200 on retry (confirmed reachable).

data_prose_consistency.details: Covered by test.js. wins.json length=71 (69 dome WINs + WIN-057b/058b paired sub-entries). llms.txt cites '71 WIN entries documenting all 69 dome WIN numbers' — matches. Verdict tally (Refuted 9, Self-Contradicted 14, Misleading 22, Std-Model 18, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4) sums to 71.

win_detail_consistency.details: Covered by test.js. Build regenerated cleanly with no diff against published HTML.

win_dome_alignment.details: Fetched john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html (91.7 KB). Dome unique WIN ids: 69. Our normalized ids (excluding b-suffixes): 69. Full coverage — no missing or extra WIN numbers. Heavy-mention duplicates on dome side (WIN-062 ×7, WIN-004 ×6, WIN-057/-058/-007 ×5) reflect the dome's own multi-section listing, already understood and tracked separately.

progressive_disclosure.details: Covered by test.js Section 8 (Prediction Panels) and Section 7 (Prose Content Integrity). All checks passed.

hardcoded_theme_colors.details: Scanned data/sections.json for light-theme inline color literals (#fff, #ffffff, #f8f9fa, etc.) — none found.

discoverability_infrastructure.details: llms.txt (5,628 bytes) — present, references funwithscience.net/dome-model-review, mentions 71 WIN entries / 69 dome numbers / 0 surviving claims, all consistent with current data. sitemap.xml — valid XML with main URL + llms.txt entry. robots.txt — present with Allow:/ + Sitemap: directive. uncounted-failures.json — 10 entries (FAIL-001 through FAIL-010), no duplicates, schema valid (id, dome_ref_*, prediction_name, what_actually_happened all present). HTML head has meta description, og:title, og:description, og:type, twitter:card, and one application/ld+json block containing ClaimReview.

expansion_tracker_continuity.details: items=279, next_id=281, max_id=280. Static next_id check passes (281 > 280). HOWEVER an orphaned output file EXP-281.json (11,284 bytes, created 2026-04-30T08:00, committed in workspace-sync d1cbf10) exists on disk with NO matching tracker entry — the analyst Mode 2b singleton run wrote the EXP file and a proposal but never appended {id: 'EXP-281'} into items[] or incremented next_id to 282. Two consequences: (a) the decider will not see EXP-281 on the tracker for integration; (b) the next allocation of next_id=281 will collide with the existing EXP-281.json on disk. EXP-052 historical gap remains (renumbered to EXP-054, benign). Stale pending: 0. All other complete entries with no output_file have a no_output_file_reason field.

workspace_only_files.details: Scratch/temp clutter on FUSE not in git: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (empty 0-byte file, accidental tab-completion), monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak (legacy backup), and 8 ad-hoc text files at monitor/analyst/ root (GLOBE-AUDIT-EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY.txt, __test_fuse.tmp, globe-dependencies-inventory.txt, latest-analysis-summary.txt, origin-theory.md, python-files-inventory.txt, wgs84-dependency-audit.md, wgs84-detailed-findings.txt). None are pipeline outputs — no real review/expansion work is at risk of silent data loss. Empty new-wins/ directory exists in git but not on FUSE (cosmetic).

priority_queue_coupling.details: All 0 coupled-category items (section-new + category-proposal-writeup, status != integrated) have priority-queue entries. No decoupled items.

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: Last 24h: 132 mtime-guard skips (informational, normal — git version newer than FUSE for shared multi-writer files like CAT-005.json, daily-report-2026-04-14T23-13.json, applied-patches/*.json). 0 direction-violation skips. No writer-on-wrong-side bugs.

digest_pipeline_coverage.details: Regenerated digest in clean clone (exit 0). Total reviews: 241 unique base IDs across 435 files. Processed: 428. Unprocessed: 6 (EXP-259-SEC1.8-WIN016.c1, EXP-277-section-rewrite, SEC-4.2.7a-EXP-276.c2, SEC-4.2.7a.c1, WIN-001.c6, WIN-044.c5). Within normal pipeline lag (<10). Mixed prefix distribution (EXP/SEC/WIN — no filter-bug regression).

broken_review_files.details: Mandatory digest regeneration ran cleanly. errors[] empty in pending-digest.json. 0 parse-errored review files.

build_reproducibility.details: Cloned to /tmp/dome-integrity-clone, ran node build.js html, diffed against pre-build docs/index.html. Output identical (0 diff lines). Build is reproducible from data files.

issues_found.description: EXP-281.json exists on disk and is committed to git, but expansion-tracker.json has no items[] entry for EXP-281 and next_id is still 281. The analyst Mode 2b singleton run today (2026-04-30T08:02:57Z, last_analyst_note in review-state.json describes the work) wrote the EXP file plus a proposal-SEC-4.2.7-20x-wording-precision file, but skipped the tracker bookkeeping. Two fallout risks: (a) decider will not see EXP-281 on the tracker for integration of the one-substring '~20×' wording fix in §4.2.7; (b) next analyst allocation of next_id=281 will collide with the existing on-disk file.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider should self-heal during next run: append {id: 'EXP-281', status: 'pending', category: 'minor_edit_followup', target: 'data/sections.json part4 §4.2.7', source_iss: 'ISS-1742', output_file: 'monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-281.json', created_at: '2026-04-30T08:00Z'} into items[] and bump next_id to 282. Alternatively, after integrating the EXP-281 patch, mark status=integrated. Worth investigating why the analyst Mode 2b path skipped the tracker write — singleton-path handler may have a missing tracker.append call.

issues_found.description: PO.DAAC (NASA JPL) URLs time out from this sandbox at both 12s and 20s curl deadlines. Recurring across recent runs, classified as external infrastructure issue rather than broken link (no DNS failure, just connection timeout — host advertises an IP but doesn't ACK).
issues_found.location: https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/ and https://podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov/drive/files/allData/grace/docs/ProdSpecDoc_v4.6.pdf (both linked from GRACE-FO citations)
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action needed unless persists for >2 weeks. Already tracked in prior reports; ISS-1015 covers external-link generic-redirect issues in the same neighborhood. Optionally swap to https://gracefo.jpl.nasa.gov/ as the GRACE-FO landing page — already in our link set and resolves cleanly.

issues_found.description: Scratch and backup files clutter the FUSE workspace at monitor/analyst/ root and inside the curmudgeon reviews / analyst expansions directories. None are pipeline outputs (an empty .build.js file and a .bak file in append-only dirs, and 8 historical .txt/.md/.tmp files at analyst root). Not at risk of silent data loss because nothing depends on them, but they confuse workspace-sync runs and noise the workspace-only-files diagnostic.
issues_found.location: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js; monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak; monitor/analyst/{GLOBE-AUDIT-EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY.txt, __test_fuse.tmp, globe-dependencies-inventory.txt, latest-analysis-summary.txt, origin-theory.md, python-files-inventory.txt, wgs84-dependency-audit.md, wgs84-detailed-findings.txt}
issues_found.suggested_fix: Operator-side cleanup. FUSE cannot unlink, so these can only be removed by overwriting with empty content (already 0-byte for some) or by waiting for a workspace recycle. Tracked under existing workspace-clutter notes.

summary: Overall WARN. Major finding: orphan EXP-281.json on disk with no expansion-tracker entry and next_id still pointing at 281 — the analyst Mode 2b singleton run today wrote the file but skipped the tracker bookkeeping; the decider will not see the wording-precision fix for §4.2.7 (~20× shortfall claim) until tracker is patched, and the next allocation will collide with the existing file. Test suite 3513/3513, build is reproducible (0 diff), all internal links and tab structure pass, no parse-errored reviews, digest pipeline coverage clean (6 unprocessed within normal lag), no direction-violation sync skips, no broken external data links (PO.DAAC timeout is recurring infra). WIN coverage matches dome (69 numbers + 2 paired sub-entries). Discoverability infra (llms.txt, sitemap.xml, robots.txt, meta tags, ClaimReview ld+json) all healthy.
